title: The Stone-Root Convergence

features.name: root-learning-shelter
features.desc-on-touch: a small shed buried so deep that root and stone have built it together. neither the root nor the mason knew what they were making.

features.name: stone-archive-foundation
features.desc-on-touch: here the archive learned to store things that would outlast forgetting. stone because it remembers by refusing to change. the first lesson.

features.name: root-crown-descent-marker
features.desc-on-touch: the roots come down and the stone comes up. at this meeting point, the archive stopped trying to decide which is which.

features.name: convergence-shrine
features.desc-on-touch: built from stone and held by root, or built by root and held by stone. the distinction has never mattered here. this place remembers both, equally.

features.name: glyph-carved-by-time
features.desc-on-touch: you cannot tell if the root carved this into the stone, or the stone wore the root into this shape. the archive's second lesson: patience unlearns direction.

inscription: the first keeper learned that stone and root, fighting, make a home. the second keeper learned they were never fighting. the archive lives in that peace.

file.name: Root_Stone_Growth_Pattern_Study_Undated.txt
file.note: sketches of a tree root and quarried stone photographed side by side. identical grain patterns. no photographer's mark. no date. handwriting suggests it was never meant to be found.

reverie.title: REVERIE — the stone-root meeting in the red earth
narr: you kneel where Katita has marked the hole, three feet down, where
the casing will rest between root and stone. she says: root comes
first, then stone refuses to move, then both decide they are the same.
you hand her the casing. her hands are red earth. she says: the trees
here know something we don't. we're just learning their alphabet.
you realize later: she meant to keep learning it, with you, forever.

exits.label: back west and up to where the crown is still root
east into stone-only country where root has never reached
deeper, where stone remembers being sand and root remembers being seed
up toward the archive's sleeping level
